title: Enable Real-Time AI for High-Speed Data Acquisition with DAQIRI | NVIDIA Technical Blog
gist: NVIDIA introduced DAQIRI, a Holoscan-platform networking library that streams high-bandwidth detector data directly into GPU memory (kernel-bypass, zero-copy) for real-time AI inference, shifting scientific data acquisition from a hardware-centric 'collect-store-analyze' model to a software-defined edge-AI pipeline. CERN's A-GHOST project uses it to run AI on HL-LHC collision data that current systems discard.

insights.claim: NVIDIA DAQIRI reframes scientific data acquisition as a software-defined problem, replacing inflexible hardware-centric collection with an adaptable pipeline that pre-processes data at the source to mitigate information loss from the data deluge.
insights.speaker: Cara Laasch
insights.so_what: Positions NVIDIA edge compute as the new gatekeeper of scientific and industrial sensor data; expands GPU TAM into instrumentation markets previously owned by FPGA/ASIC vendors.
insights.quote: NVIDIA DAQIRI (Data Acquisition for Integrated Real-time Instruments) shifts the data acquisition to an adaptable, software-centric architecture from an inflexible hardware-centric design.

insights.claim: The bottleneck in modern science has shifted from missing data to the legacy 'collect, store, analyze' architecture, which was never designed for sources like LCLS-II that generate photon pulses at a 1 MHz repetition rate.
insights.speaker: Cara Laasch
insights.so_what: A structural bottleneck shift creates a fresh capex cycle for edge-AI ingest hardware; watch instrument-maker adoption as the demand signal.
insights.quote: output rates continue to increase and shift the bottleneck away from missing data to the current “collect, store, analyze” architecture, which has never been designed to deal with high data rates on short time scales.

insights.claim: DAQIRI completely bypasses the Linux kernel using DPDK to route data zero-copy directly from a ConnectX NIC into GPU DMA buffers, keeping latency at PCIe transit time while handling UDP and RoCE v2 at line rates of hundreds of Gbps.
insights.speaker: Cara Laasch
insights.so_what: Bundling ConnectX NICs with GPU ingest pulls NVIDIA's networking (ex-Mellanox) franchise into scientific/industrial sales; networking attach is a margin-accretive cross-sell.
insights.quote: DAQIRI provides zero-copy access, routing data directly from the NIC to the GPU’s Direct Memory Access (DMA) buffers.
insights.locator: How DAQIRI works under the hood

insights.claim: DAQIRI performs data-type conversion during the GPU reorder step, e.g. converting compact int4 samples on the wire into fp16 tensors, avoiding a separate unpacking pass in the application.
insights.speaker: Cara Laasch
insights.so_what: On-the-fly precision conversion makes detector streams immediately model-ready, lowering the integration cost that has kept GPUs out of front-line data acquisition.
insights.quote: a sensor frontend may send compact int4 samples on the wire, while the GPU processing or AI inference stage expects fp16.
insights.locator: DAQIRI applications start with a configuration file

insights.claim: DAQIRI abstracts low-level networking behind YAML configuration files plus C++/Python APIs and ties into TensorRT and nvCOMP, letting instrument builders add inference, filtering and compression without modifying their instruments.
insights.speaker: Cara Laasch
insights.so_what: Low integration friction plus the broader Holoscan/TensorRT pull-through is how NVIDIA converts a networking library into full-stack platform lock-in for scientific edge AI.
insights.quote: This allows developers to focus entirely on writing their custom inference or filtering logic rather than managing network protocols.
insights.locator: While the underlying data movement relies on low-level networking optimizations

insights.claim: CERN's A-GHOST project uses DAQIRI to run AI-driven searches on the HL-LHC collision stream that the nominal selection path discards, applying Convolutional Auto-Encoders, temporal CNNs and transformer models in real time.
insights.speaker: Cara Laasch
insights.so_what: A marquee CERN deployment is a powerful credibility anchor for selling DAQIRI into other big-science and industrial instrument markets.
insights.quote: The A-GHOST project uses DAQIRI to apply more powerful AI-driven searches to the stream that is discarded by the nominal selection path

insights.claim: The HL-LHC upgrade raises luminosity tenfold and lifts the ATLAS first-stage selected-event bandwidth to 1 MHz (from 100 kHz) and second-stage to 10 kHz (from 1 kHz), yet still rejects more than 99% of all collisions online.
insights.speaker: Cara Laasch
insights.so_what: Quantifies the discarded-data opportunity DAQIRI targets; the >99% rejection rate is the size of the addressable problem driving GPU-near-detector demand.
insights.quote: Even at this increased rate, this still implies rejecting more than 99% of all collisions in the online system.

insights.claim: The A-GHOST architecture connects custom FPGA-based detector boards directly to a high-performance GPU processing farm, led by CERN Openlab, University of Chicago and UCL.
insights.speaker: Cara Laasch
insights.so_what: FPGA-to-GPU streaming shows NVIDIA complementing rather than replacing front-end FPGAs, a coexistence model that eases adoption in instrumentation.

insights.claim: DAQIRI scales across NVIDIA's edge-supercomputing line from DGX Spark to IGX to rack-based RTX Pro Server or VR200 depending on the instrument's compute needs.
insights.speaker: Cara Laasch
insights.so_what: A tiered hardware ladder lets NVIDIA monetize the same DAQIRI software across price points, maximizing attach across the edge-AI portfolio.
insights.quote: These edge supercomputing systems can range in size from the NVIDIA DGX Spark to the NVIDIA IGX Platform to node and rack-based solutions like NVIDIA RTX Pro Server or VR200
insights.locator: By streaming instrument outputs directly into edge supercomputing capabilities
